statement.title: Statement
statement.reminder: A statement is one instruction. It almost always ends with a semicolon ;

semicolon.title: Semicolon ;
semicolon.reminder: Ends a statement. Forget one and Apex will refuse to compile.

variable-declaration.title: Variable shape
variable-declaration.reminder: TYPE name = value;  — three parts in order. Example: Integer count = 0;

type.title: Type
type.reminder: What KIND of value a variable holds. Apex requires you to declare the type.

string-quotes.title: String quotes
string-quotes.reminder: Apex strings use SINGLE quotes only — 'hello', not "hello".

camel-case.title: camelCase
camel-case.reminder: Variables and methods: lowercase first word, capitalize each next word. myAge, isLearning.

pascal-case.title: PascalCase
pascal-case.reminder: Types and class names: capitalize EVERY word, including the first. Integer, Account, MyHelper.

case-insensitive.title: Apex is case-insensitive
case-insensitive.reminder: myVariable and MyVariable are the same to the compiler — but pick one style and be consistent.

block.title: Block { }
block.reminder: Curly braces group code that runs together — class bodies, method bodies, if-blocks, loops.

assignment-operator.title: = is assignment
assignment-operator.reminder: A single = puts the value on the right INTO the variable on the left. == is comparison (later).

string-concat.title: + joins strings
string-concat.reminder: Use + to concatenate strings: 'Hello, ' + name. Same operator that adds numbers.

method-shape.title: Method anatomy
method-shape.reminder: modifier static? returnType name(params) { body } — e.g. public static void doIt() { ... }

void.title: void
void.reminder: A method whose return type is void returns nothing — it just runs.

public-modifier.title: public
public-modifier.reminder: An access modifier — public means any other class can use this. Alternatives: private, protected.

static-vs-instance.title: static vs instance
static-vs-instance.reminder: static = belongs to the class itself, called as ClassName.method(). Instance = belongs to one object, called as obj.method().

parameter-vs-argument.title: Parameter vs Argument
parameter-vs-argument.reminder: Parameter = the named slot in the method definition. Argument = the actual value you pass when calling.

class-vs-object.title: Class vs Object
class-vs-object.reminder: Class = the blueprint. Object (instance) = one actual thing built from it.
